name: National Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn shop chain. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need immediate liquidity without traditional credit requirements. FirstCash has grown to operate more than 3,300 retail pawn locations across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador), employing approximately 22,000 people globally.

The company's core business model centers on pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property—alongside retail buying and selling of merchandise. FirstCash purchases and sells jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other merchandise for cash. They also operate additional services including gold and precious metals buying, layaway plans (with 10% down payment), and through their subsidiary AFF, lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions via 15,000+ merchant partner locations.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive geographic footprint and scale as the market leader in pawn lending. The company is publicly traded and indexed in both the S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000, indicating institutional-grade operations and transparency. Their "store locator" and "inventory" features suggest technology-enabled operations beyond typical pawn shops, and they actively promote gold buying and layaway services as customer value-adds.

For consumers, FirstCash provides immediate cash access without credit checks, making it viable for those with poor credit or no credit history. However, pawn loans inherently carry the risk of losing pledged items if not repaid, and retail markups on merchandise can be substantial. The company's size and structure make it a reliable alternative to independent pawn shops, though terms and interest rates should be compared carefully before pledging valuables.
pros: 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America—largest pawn chain with extensive geographic coverage
No credit check required; immediate cash approval based on collateral value, not creditworthiness
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) with transparent financial reporting and regulatory compliance
Multiple revenue streams beyond pawn loans: gold/precious metals buying, layaway service (10% down), retail merchandise sales
15,000+ retail merchant partners through AFF subsidiary for lease-to-own and payment solutions
Non-recourse pawn loans—borrowers are not personally liable if they default; they simply lose the pledged item
Wide merchandise acceptance (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
cons: Risk of losing personal property permanently if unable to repay the pawn loan within the loan term
Pawn loan interest rates and fees are not disclosed on website; rates can be significantly higher than traditional personal loans
Retail markup on merchandise for sale is typically substantial, making purchases more expensive than other retailers
Limited transparency on loan terms, repayment periods, and renewal policies on the main website
Layaway service requires 10% upfront payment with no mention of whether remaining balance is interest-free
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit history who need immediate cash and have valuable items to pledge
People in temporary cash flow crunches who expect to recover funds within weeks and can safely recover their collateral
Individuals selling jewelry, gold, electronics, or tools for quick cash without waiting for online marketplaces
Bargain hunters shopping for discounted used electronics, instruments, or merchandise (accepting higher markup vs. retail)
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for credit-constrained consumers who own valuable items and need immediate cash without a credit check, or bargain shoppers willing to pay retail markups for used merchandise. The main caveat is that pawn loans require pledging personal property as collateral—failure to repay results in permanent loss of the item—and rates/fees are not transparently disclosed upfront, making rate comparison essential before borrowing.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (non-recourse)
Gold and precious metals buying (cash on the spot)
Retail sales of used/recovered merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments)
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Store locator and inventory browsing online
Lease-to-own payment solutions through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail finance payment solutions through AFF
Consumer lending focused on cash-constrained populations
Multi-national pawn operations (U.S., UK, Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, El Salvador)
